ED_RELIANCE_BY_CONTINUITY.description: Patients who consistently see the same physician for 70%+ of their care have the lowest rate of minor emergency room visits.
Patients with a designated doctor but who frequently seek care elsewhere have a 76% higher rate of minor-condition ER visits.
Patients with very fragmented relationships with their physician have a nearly 3x increase in minor-condition ER visits.
Unattached patients have no ongoing family doctor and must rely on Walk-in clinics or Emergency Departments for minor symptoms.
Provincial average rate of minor (CTAS 4/5) emergency department visits.

PCN_CAPACITY.source: Alberta Primary Care Networks (approximate from PCN/PCA public reports)
PCN_CAPACITY.verification: PCN structure is real but transitioning to 7 Regional Primary Health Care Networks under PCA. Specific provider/enrollment numbers are plausible but could not be confirmed against a single published source. PCNs are being restructured.

ED_RELIANCE_BY_CONTINUITY.verification: CIHI publishes continuity-of-care research showing lower ED reliance for patients with high continuity. Specific rates are plausible but exact figures could not be confirmed against a specific publication.

CONTINUITY_SATISFACTION.verification: CIHI Shared Health Priorities indicators include continuity-of-care and access measures. Zone-level percentages are plausible but could not be confirmed against a specific published table.
